cards.back: A computer system that mimics the decision-making ability of a human expert in a particular field

cards.front: Myths and Paradoxes of Expert Systems
cards.back: A set of problems and challenges that were initially thought to be inherent but were later overcome with the development of more advanced AI techniques
The process of using a model trained for one task as a starting point for a new, but related task
The process of organizing and structuring knowledge into a format that can be used by a computer system
Front-end refers to the user interface and client-side logic of an application, while back-end refers to the server-side logic and database
